Title: Integrated Analysis of GC–MS-Based Metabolomics and Proteomics Reveals the Importance of Volatile Metabolite Stigmasterol in the Defence Response of Panax notoginseng Against Root Rot

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a C-22 double bond into β-sitosterol to produce stigmasterol.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for stigmasterol accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes stigmasterol accumulation and enhances resistance to Fusarium solani; RNAi reduces stigmasterol and increases susceptibility, whereas overexpression increases stigmasterol and resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defence metabolite; inhibits spore germination and hyphal growth of Fusarium solani; associated with maintenance of membrane integrity and antioxidant/defence functions.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots; also measured in leaves and transgenic tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 2-year-old plants; WT and transgenic tobacco T2 lines A1, A6, A8, A11
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in various organs, highest in stems and roots, lowest in flowers; induced by Fusarium solani and MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In P. notoginseng, induced in roots by Fusarium solani and signalling molecules; in transgenic tobacco, highly expressed in overexpression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family 710 subfamily A; conserved C-22 sterol desaturase domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated metabolomics/proteomics association; RT-qPCR; promoter-GUS assays; transient RNAi in Panax notoginseng; stable overexpression in Nicotiana tabacum; HPLC quantification of stigmasterol; subcellular localization; pathogen inoculation assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium solani infection; methyl jasmonate (MeJA); ethephon; gibberellin 3; salicylic acid; abscisic acid; hydrogen peroxide; drought; cold; injury; AlCl3; NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnCYP710A was identified by integrated proteomics and metabolomics as an induced steroid biosynthesis enzyme associated with increased stigmasterol under Fusarium solani infection. 2. PnCYP710A encodes a cytoplasmic CYP710A sterol C-22 desaturase expressed mainly in stems and roots and inducible by MeJA and pathogen infection. 3. RNAi of PnCYP710A in Panax notoginseng reduced stigmasterol to 66% of control, increased lesion area about fivefold, suppressed JA-pathway genes, reduced lignin and callose deposition, and impaired ROS homeostasis. 4. Overexpression in tobacco increased stigmasterol up to 1.53-fold, enhanced resistance to Fusarium solani, up-regulated JA-related genes, increased lignin/callose deposition, and improved ROS scavenging.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC–MS metabolomics; TMT quantitative proteomics (LC–MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to PnCYP710A as a key downstream steroid synthesis gene and as a stigmasterol synthetase gene, but its established biochemical role is sterol C-22 desaturation converting β-sitosterol to stigmasterol. Functional effects were tested in both native transient RNAi system and heterologous tobacco overexpression system.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering or selecting root-rot-resistant Panax notoginseng or other crops by elevating stigmasterol biosynthesis; could be stacked with defence regulators such as PnWRKY4 or JA-pathway components.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant growth or morphology difference reported in PnCYP710A-overexpressing tobacco lines.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates PnCYP710A transcription, thereby promoting stigmasterol biosynthesis and defence against Fusarium solani.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strongest tested WRKY activator of the PnCYP710A promoter among PnWRKY2/4/5/9/12/15/27; acts as a positive upstream regulator of stigmasterol accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PnCYP710A promoter transcription by binding W-box elements.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defence metabolite inhibiting Fusarium solani growth and germination.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified among differentially expressed transcription factors in proteomic/correlation analysis during Fusarium solani infection.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fusarium solani infection; defence-associated induction inferred from DETF analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis with steroid accumulation; transient co-expression and GUS assay in tobacco; stable co-expression assay in tobacco; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Correlation analysis implicated WRKY factors in stigmasterol regulation during Fusarium solani infection. 2. Among seven tested PnWRKY proteins, PnWRKY4 most strongly activated the PnCYP710A promoter, increasing GUS activity about 3.4-fold in transient assay. 3. In planta co-expression further increased PPnCYP710A-driven GUS activity by 17.61-fold relative to promoter-only control. 4. Y1H and dual-luciferase assays demonstrated direct binding and transcriptional activation of the PnCYP710A promoter by PnWRKY4.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: TMT quantitative proteomics-derived DETF identification; correlation analysis with GC–MS metabolomics; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Several other WRKYs (PnWRKY2/5/9/12/15/27) also positively affected the promoter, but PnWRKY4 was selected and experimentally validated as the main regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulatory target for boosting stigmasterol-mediated disease resistance, potentially combined with PnCYP710A overexpression or promoter editing of W-box responsiveness.
